examples: Such anomalies are due to the relative abundance of the "isotopes" or varieties of each element.
The isotopes of a given element have the same chemical properties but slightly different physical properties.
Most hydrogen and oxygen atoms in water are stable, but traces of both elements are also present as heavier isotopes.
Once the reactor has shut down, there remains "decay heat" from traces of other radioactive isotopes.
Resulting electrical pulses are analyzed by software that can determine which isotopes are present.
